issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-bottom > p
issuesFound.explanation: The footer bottom paragraph uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) (#64748b) against background: var(--colour-neutral-900) (#0f172a), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.3:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements for normal text (requires 4.5:1).

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating with Tab will not see any focus indicator on focused elements, making it impossible to track their current location.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 36px with vertical padding of only 6px (0.375rem), resulting in a touch target below the 44px recommended size and insufficient padding to compensate. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating the button.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links set min-height: 24px without sufficient padding to reach the 44px recommended touch target size. The inline-block display with only color transition provides no additional hit area.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring because the outline width is zero. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and width: auto/height: auto, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This breaks the intended accessibility pattern.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, creating a touch target well below the 44px recommended size and the 24px minimum. This makes activation difficult for users with tremors or limited precision.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. Users relying on touch input may struggle to toggle the checkbox without accidentally activating adjacent controls.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class includes transform in its transition property to animate the :active state (translateY(1px)), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this movement for users with vestibular disorders.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The primary CTA button includes transform in its transition for hover effects (translateY(-1px)), but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to suppress this spatial movement for users who have requested reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on headings
issuesFound.explanation: The h1-h6 group selector sets letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) (-0.025em), which reduces letter spacing below normal. This can reduce readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .hero-content h1
issuesFound.explanation: The hero heading uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tighter) (-0.05em), which tightens text significantly and can impair readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .section-header h2
issuesFound.explanation: The section header h2 uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) (-0.025em), reducing readability by compressing character spacing.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .step-content h3
issuesFound.explanation: The step content heading uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) (-0.025em), which can make text harder to read for users with visual impairments.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .products-section .section-header h2
issuesFound.explanation: The products section header uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) (-0.025em), reducing legibility.

issuesFound.explanation: The pricing section header uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) (-0.025em), which compresses text and reduces readability.

issuesFound.explanation: The pricing section header paragraph uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against background: var(--colour-bg-subtle) (#f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .contact-section > p
issuesFound.explanation: The contact section paragraph uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against background: var(--colour-bg-subtle) (#f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.1:1.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .footer-brand address

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove underlines with text-decoration: none and rely solely on color change (var(--colour-neutral-700) to var(--colour-brand-primary)) to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links use text-decoration: none with only a color transition on hover, providing no non-colour visual indicator that these are links. Users with colour vision deficiencies may not perceive the links.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, which renders no visible focus ring because offset applies to a zero-width outline. Keyboard users cannot see which element is focused, blocking navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, removing the visible focus ring for button elements. Keyboard users cannot identify focus state on interactive buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 44x44 CSS pixels for interactive elements. Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate buttons.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the recommended 44px touch target size. Small buttons are difficult to tap accurately on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px × 28px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum and far below the 44px recommended size. This makes the button extremely difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is sized to 20px × 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.2 minimum target size of 24px. Users with dexterity limitations may fail to toggle the checkbox.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. Links in the footer are often small and hard to tap on mobile.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content fully visible and in-flow instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible. This breaks the intended screen-reader-only pattern.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 597 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the animation.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #f8fafc ratio 2.9:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint class uses #aaaaaa text on a light background (inherited #f8fafc), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text, making hints hard to read.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed in forced-colors mode without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline from :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative such as a border or box-shadow. Users in Windows High Contrast Mode lose all focus indication.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and only provides outline-offset, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible and 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

Line 180: Remove the line or change to:
    word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector removes the outline and provides only an outline-offset with no visible replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, clip: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible in the layout and does not hide it from visual users. Conversely, it fails to properly hide content visually while keeping it for screen readers as intended.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. No visible alternative is provided, making the mobile navigation toggle inaccessible via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 428 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still experience this animation.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can cause text to overlap or become illegible for users with cognitive disabilities or when text spacing is adjusted by user stylesheets.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #5588bb / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses #5588bb text on #ffffff background (via variables). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.76:1, which fails WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration (underline) without providing another non-colour visual differentiator. Links are indistinguishable from surrounding text for users who cannot perceive colour.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links remove underlines and only use colour/background changes on hover. Colour alone is insufficient to indicate interactivity for some users.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links remove underlines and rely on colour change on hover. This fails SC 1.4.1 Use of Colour.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-lg min-height 36px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Large buttons should be easier to hit, not smaller.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width 20px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle min-height 28px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a min-height 28px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width 28px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .filter-tabs button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons set min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Users switching filters may mis-tap.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .product-body .btn-secondary min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Product card buttons set min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .pricing-toggle button min-height 28px

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width 20px

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The rule #site-search label.sr-only sets display: none, which completely removes the label from the accessibility tree. Screen reader users will not know the purpose of the search input.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform: translateY(-200%) with a transition. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 423 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 423: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform. There is no prefers-reduced-motion override that disables this transform transition for buttons.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces the space between words, making text significantly harder to read for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .input-hint
issuesFound.explanation: The .input-hint text color is #aaaaaa on a white background (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.8:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-bottom > p

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: The .stat-label text color is rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) on a gradient background. 30% opacity white text provides extremely low contrast, likely below 3:1, making it unreadable.
issuesFound.fix: Line 974: .stat-label { color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. Links throughout the site rely on color alone to be distinguished from text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 unless underlined or otherwise indicated on hover/focus.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a

issuesFound.fix: Line 1141: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social links have text-decoration: none and rely on color and image opacity changes on hover. Text links should have underlines or borders to be distinguishable by more than color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1153: .social-links a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible in forced-colors mode

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, which does not create a visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus. outline-offset alone does not render a visible indicator.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Navigation links, footer links, and other body-text links rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 201: `a { text-decoration: underline; }` or add `a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }` and ensure non-hover states have border-bottom or other indicators.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule-block. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements, blocking keyboard navigation.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector inside @media (forced-colors: active) sets outline: none without a visible alternative. This breaks focus visibility specifically for users in high-contrast mode.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule-block. Keyboard users cannot see focus on buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .header-inner > a:first-child:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The logo link focus-visible state removes the outline without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on the logo link.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile nav toggle button focus-visible state removes the outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on this control.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links remove the outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot track focus within submenus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input focus-visible rule sets outline: none without re-affirming the box-shadow in the same block, risking loss of focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button focus-visible state removes the outline without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on this button.

issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on these links.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8). This makes buttons difficult to activate on touch devices.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes small buttons extremely difficult to activate.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-lg min-height 36px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height to 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons are particularly hard to hit at this size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle min-height/min-width 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button sets min-height and min-width to 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: [id$="-submenu"] li a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Submenu links set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width and height to 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .hero-ctas .btn-primary min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: Hero CTA buttons set min-height: 32px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .hero-ctas .btn-secondary min-height 32px
issuesFound.explanation: Hero secondary buttons set min-height: 32px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .product-body .btn-secondary min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Product card buttons set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .pricing-toggle button min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes set width and height to 20px, below the 24px minimum size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a min-height 24px
issuesFound.explanation: Footer nav links set min-height: 24px. While meeting the 24px minimum, this is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .social-links a min-height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: Social links set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto. This makes the content visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible, breaking the intended screen-reader-only pattern.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses transform and transition for animation. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 403 is empty and does not disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable this motion for affected users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img uses transform and transition for a 3D effect. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 723 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .required-note foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 3.5:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .required-note text color (#aaaaaa) on the page background (#ffffff) has a contrast ratio of approximately 3.5:1. This fails WCAG AA (4.5:1) for normal text (14px).

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none. Many links rely solely on color to distinguish themselves from text, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) for users who cannot perceive color differences.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. This relies on color alone to indicate interactivity.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links have text-decoration: none and only change color on hover. This relies on color alone to indicate interactivity.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the outline on :focus-visible without providing a visible alternative. Users in Windows High Contrast Mode will lose focus indicators entirely, making keyboard navigation impossible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 377: `@media (forced-colors: active) { :focus-visible { outline: 2px solid ButtonText; outline-offset: 3px; } }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn-sm sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may struggle to activate these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 756: `.btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a sets min-height: 24px, which meets the minimum but is below the 44px recommended target size. The padding is insufficient to reach 44px.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1448: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible removes the outline with no replacement box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating dropdown menus will not see which link has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 570: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class incorrectly implemented
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .sr-only uses position: static and auto dimensions, which makes the content visible in the layout rather than hiding it visually while keeping it for screen readers. This breaks the utility class purpose and may expose visual content incorrectly.
issuesFound.fix: Line 394: `.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on navigation toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .nav-toggle sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px with padding: var(--space-2) (6px), resulting in a total height of approximately 40px. This is below the 44px recommended target size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520: `.nav-toggle { display: none; min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: var(--space-2); background: none; border: 1px solid var(--colour-border); border-radius: var(--radius-md); color: var(--colour-text-primary); font-size: var(--font-size-xl); cursor: pointer; transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn-icon sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon-only buttons are particularly difficult to hit when undersized.
issuesFound.fix: Line 768: `.btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .form-group input[type="checkbox"] sets width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Users with motor impairments may struggle to toggle these controls.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1435: `.form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }`

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body text
issuesFound.explanation: The selector body sets word-spacing: -0.2em and letter-spacing: -0.05em. Negative word-spacing reduces the visual separation between words, making text harder to read for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 347: `body { min-height: 100%; font-family: var(--font-family-sans); font-size: var(--font-size-base); font-weight: var(--font-weight-normal); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); -webkit-font-smoothing: antialiased; -moz-osx-font-smoothing: grayscale; text-rendering: optimizeLegibility; overflow-x: hidden; position: relative; word-spacing: normal; letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-normal); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on main navigation items
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button sets min-height: 28px with vertical padding of var(--space-2) (6px), totaling approximately 40px. This is below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: `#nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-1); padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); font-weight: var(--font-weight-medium); color: var(--colour-neutral-700); text-decoration: none; border-radius: var(--radius-md); border: none; background: none; cursor: pointer; transition: color var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); white-space: nowrap; min-height: 32px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #search-input sets height: 28px with vertical padding var(--space-2) (6px), resulting in a total height of approximately 40px. This is below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 590: `#search-input { width: 220px; height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); padding-right: var(--space-10); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-text-primary); background-color: var(--colour-neutral-50); border: 1px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-full); transition: border-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast), background-color var(--transition-fast); appearance: none; -webkit-appearance: none; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible removes the outline without replacement. Keyboard users cannot track focus position in the account navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 635: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users will not see focus on the search button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 619: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn:focus-visible removes the outline with no visible alternative. All button elements using this class will lack focus indicators for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 690: `.btn:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on feature card links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .feature-card a:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a replacement visual indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which feature card link is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 831: `.feature-card a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .filter-tabs button:focus-visible removes the outline without replacement. Keyboard users navigating the filter tab group lose focus visibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1043: `.filter-tabs button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on table wrapper
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .table-wrapper:focus-visible removes the outline without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the scrollable table region has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1254: `.table-wrapper:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .faq-item dt button:focus-visible sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative. Keyboard users navigating the FAQ accordion cannot see focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1376: `.faq-item dt button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) (#555555) from the footer. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.1:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1448: `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-300); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 24px; }` (where --colour-neutral-300 is #cbd5e1 for ~3.1:1 contrast, or better use --colour-neutral-200 #e2e8f0 for ~4.6:1)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .social-links a:focus-visible sets outline: none with no visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see focus on social media links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1471: `.social-links a:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .social-links a sets min-height: 28px with vertical padding var(--space-2) (6px), totaling approximately 40px. This is below the 44px recommended target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1467: `.social-links a { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: var(--space-2); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); min-height: 32px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible base rule
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating the page will not see any focus indicator on interactive elements, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: `:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The selector footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a:focus-visible removes the outline without replacement. Keyboard users navigating footer links lose focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on search button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"] sets width: 28px and height: 28px with no padding, resulting in a 28px touch target. This is below the 44px recommended size and close to the 24px minimum.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on filter tab buttons

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on pricing toggle buttons

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .btn includes transform in its transition property but lacks a @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to disable the transform animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the movement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 700: (add after line 700) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTAs
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .hero-ctas .btn-primary includes transform in its transition but has no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override to suppress the transform animation. This can cause issues for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 773: (add after line 773) `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on active pricing toggle button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] uses color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff) against background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.9:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1138: `.pricing-toggle button[aria-pressed="true"] { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); }` (where --colour-brand-primary-hover is #004aaa for ~7.5:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on active filter tab button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .filter-tabs button[aria-pressed="true"] uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) against background-color: var(--colour-bg-page) (#ffffff). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.9:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1047: `.filter-tabs button[aria-pressed="true"] { background-color: var(--colour-bg-page); color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); font-weight: var(--font-weight-semibold); box-shadow: var(--shadow-sm); }` (where --colour-brand-primary-hover is #004aaa for ~7.0:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on current page navigation link
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #nav-menu > li > a[aria-current="page"] uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) against background-color: var(--colour-info-light) (#dbeafe). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.4:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on navigation hover state
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #nav-menu > li > a:hover uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) against background-color: var(--colour-info-light) (#dbeafe). The contrast ratio is approximately 2.4:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 553: `#nav-menu > li > a:hover, #nav-menu > li > button:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); background-color: var(--colour-info-light); }`

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on submenu hover state
issuesFound.explanation: The selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:hover uses color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-100) (#f1f5f9). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.6:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() function in srUtils adds an error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Without aria-invalid, screen reader users cannot identify which fields have failed validation. The markValid() function correctly removes aria-invalid, but the corresponding set operation is missing in markInvalid().
issuesFound.fix: Line 1244: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after the addDescribedBy call inside the markInvalid() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to display the navigation drawer but never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen readers cannot programmatically determine that the menu is expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value). The close() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating an inconsistent state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1764: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger element (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state of the disclosure widget. The paired _closeInstance() function correctly sets aria-expanded="false".
issuesFound.fix: Line 1844: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;` inside the _openInstance() function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter._syncTabAriaStates() missing aria-pressed on filter tabs
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncTabAriaStates() function updates CSS classes for active/inactive filter tab buttons but does not set aria-pressed="true" on the active button or aria-pressed="false" on inactive buttons. Since these buttons act as toggle buttons controlling the filter state, they must communicate their pressed state to assistive technologies per WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2759: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after the toggleClass call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the open() function animates the panel and removes the hidden attribute, but does not update the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Although the init() function sets the initial state, dynamic updates are not communicated to screen readers, causing state mismatch for accordion items toggled after page load.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3124: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;` inside the open() function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch._open() missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The _open() function in siteSearch sets internal state to open the suggestion listbox but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the combobox input element (_inputEl). The suggestionListRenderer.mount() initializes aria-expanded="false", and _close() correctly sets it back to "false", but the opening transition lacks the attribute update, breaking the ARIA combobox pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2569: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;` inside the _open() function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch where screen users are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The corresponding close function sets it to "false", leaving the expanded state incorrect for assistive technology.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open() function expands the answer panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function sets it to "false", causing screen readers to announce the wrong state for the accordion trigger.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the input field. This prevents screen readers from announcing the field is invalid when validation fails in forms using inlineValidator.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions change visible content but do not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on AT are not notified when a dropdown menu opens or closes.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._onAccept() and _onDecline() functions update consent state and hide the banner but do not announce the decision to screen readers. Users are not confirmed that their preference was saved.

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play/pause state not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The testimonialsCarousel.play() and pause() functions update aria-pressed on the button but do not announce the autoplay state change to screen readers. Users are not informed if the carousel is auto-rotating or stopped.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer visibility not synchronized with aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() and close() functions toggle the drawer's visibility via CSS classes but do not toggle aria-hidden on the drawer element itself. This may allow screen readers to access hidden content or miss visible content depending on CSS implementation.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState uses aria-checked on potential button element
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState() function sets aria-checked on the toggle element. If the element is a native <button> (which is allowed by init logic), aria-checked is invalid; aria-pressed should be used for buttons, while aria-checked is for role="switch".

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() omits aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid() utility function adds the error element to aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers will not identify the field as having invalid input, violating WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2867: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the markInvalid function before or after addDescribedBy()

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module toggles the navigation drawer visibility but never updates the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state when the menu opens, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2144: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav lacks focus trap activation and initial focus management
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes or activates it when the drawer opens. Additionally, open() does not move focus to the drawer or first focusable element, and close() does not return focus to the trigger button. Keyboard users cannot navigate the mobile menu effectively.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2146: Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();` and `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });` Line 2180: Ensure `_trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });` is called before deactivating the trap or ensure the trap's returnFocus option handles this.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the open() function animates the panel and updates classes but omits setting aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close() function correctly sets it to "false", leaving the expanded state unannounced to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3328: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance() function reveals the dropdown panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance() correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetrical state management that leaves screen readers unaware when panels are expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2238: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the _openInstance function after `inst.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav drawer lacks dialog role and aria-modal
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer acts as a modal dialog but the JavaScript does not programmatically add role="dialog" or aria-modal="true" to the _drawer element when it opens. Screen readers may not treat it as a modal, allowing users to escape to background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2145: Add `setAttr(_drawer, 'role', 'dialog');` and `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-modal', 'true');` and `setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-label', 'Mobile navigation');` inside the open() function before activating the focus trap.

issuesFound.title: Focus trap event listeners never attach due to undefined SUPPORTS property

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus are opened or closed, the trigger button's aria-expanded state is not updated programmatically to match the panel visibility, violating the disclosure pattern requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _openInstance(): After `inst.open = true;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`. Line in _closeInstance(): After `inst.open = false;` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter changes not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item is expanded, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not set to 'true'. While the close function sets it to 'false', the open function only manages CSS classes and animation, leaving screen readers unaware that the panel is expanded.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `mount` function initializes the search combobox attributes but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the input. The state is only set when the list is shown/hidden later.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1800: setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

fpTitles: mobileNav drawer does not receive focus when opened
mobileNav focus not returned to trigger on close
mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded
mobileNav close function does not announce state change

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility missing aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid() function adds an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field, so screen readers do not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 850: In markInvalid(), add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.init() function discovers the trigger button (_trigger) and drawer elements but does not initialize the aria-expanded attribute to "false" on page load, leaving screen readers without initial state information.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1240: After `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` and ensure the trigger has aria-controls pointing to the drawer: `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close() function does not announce the state change to screen readers, unlike the open() function which announces "Navigation menu opened". Users are not notified when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1182: In the close() function, before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', ...);`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance() and _closeInstance() functions show and hide dropdown panels by manipulating CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but they do not update aria-expanded on the trigger buttons to reflect the open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: In _openInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line 1340: In _closeInstance(), add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` at the start of the function before the animation timeout.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu state changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus does not trigger a live region announcement, leaving screen reader users unaware that the menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1320: In _openInstance(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown opened');`. Line 1340: In _closeInstance(), add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed');` before closing the panel.

issuesFound.title: Product filter results not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The productFilter.applyFilters() function updates the visible product cards and the count badge but does not announce the results to screen reader users via a live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation utility missing aria-invalid clear on valid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markValid() function removes the error description but does not clear the aria-invalid attribute, leaving fields marked as invalid after they become valid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 870: In markValid(), add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');` after removing the describedby reference.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation focus trap not activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open() function references a focus trap variable (_trap) in close() but never initializes or activates a focus trap when opening the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the mobile menu into the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: In the open() function, after `_open = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger }); _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: Pricing period changes not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing.setPeriod() function updates price displays and billing period toggles visually, but does not announce the change to screen reader users, leaving them unaware that prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2550: After updating the plan cards in setPeriod(), add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator not setting aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: The inlineValidator._renderResult() function calls srUtils.markInvalid/markValid but does not directly set aria-invalid on the field element, and the srUtils functions also lack this (see Issues 9-10). This affects all forms using inlineValidator.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2850: In _renderResult(), when `result.valid` is false, add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`. When valid, add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');`.

issuesFound.title: Contact form validation lacks aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The contactForm._validateAll() function uses inlineValidator.validate() which fails to set aria-invalid on invalid fields (see Issue 11), affecting the name, email, subject, message, and consent fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3300: Ensure inlineValidator is fixed per Issue 11, or manually set aria-invalid in the contact form validation loop: `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` for each invalid field.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter form validation lacks aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletterForm._onSubmit() uses inlineValidator.validate() on the email field, which fails to set aria-invalid (see Issue 11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 3100: After `inlineValidator.validate(_emailEl)`, add `_emailEl.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` if validation fails, or ensure Issue 11 is fixed.

issuesFound.title: Multi-step wizard validation lacks aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The multiStepWizard._validateCurrentStep() uses inlineValidator.validate() for step validation, failing to set aria-invalid on invalid fields in the wizard steps.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3450: Ensure inlineValidator is fixed per Issue 11, or manually set aria-invalid on fields that fail validation in the wizard.

issuesFound.title: Active filter chip removal not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The activeFilterChips._onChipClick() function removes filter chips and updates the filter state but does not announce the removal action to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2250: After removing the tag or filter, add `liveRegion.announce(`Filter ${label} removed`);` where label is the chip text.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation module toggles the visibility of the drawer using CSS classes (`is-open`, `nav-open`) but fails to update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed when activating the toggle.
issuesFound.fix:
Line N: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); // inside open()
Line N: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); // inside close()

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller manages the `hidden` attribute and CSS classes for the panel but does not toggle `aria-expanded` on the trigger button when opening or closing the dropdown. This disconnects the visual state from the accessibility tree state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line N: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); // inside _openInstance()
Line N: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); // inside _closeInstance()

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion `open` function sets the panel to visible and updates the internal state but omits setting `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where screen readers may report the item as collapsed even when expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); // inside open()

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons do not update aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The product filter tabs toggle the `is-active` CSS class to indicate selection but do not update the `aria-pressed` attribute on the button elements. Assistive technologies rely on `aria-pressed` to convey the toggle state of filter buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive)); // inside _syncTabAriaStates()

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function `markInvalid` links the error message to the field via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input element itself. Without this attribute, screen readers may not announce the field as invalid upon focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); // inside markInvalid()

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The utility function `markValid` removes the error description association but does not remove or reset the `aria-invalid` attribute to "false". The field may remain flagged as invalid in the accessibility tree even after the error is resolved.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); // inside markValid()

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hides header without updating aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The sticky header controller hides the header element visually by adding a CSS class (`headerHidden`) when scrolling down, but it does not set `aria-hidden="true"`. This leaves the header navigation focusable and perceivable to screen reader users even when it is visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line N: _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); // when adding headerHidden class
Line N: _header.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); // when removing headerHidden class

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce result count change
issuesFound.explanation: When the product filter updates the grid and changes the visible product count, it updates the badge text but does not announce this change to screen readers via a live region. Users are not informed that the content has been filtered or how many results remain.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); // inside applyFilters() callback

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce billing change
issuesFound.explanation: Switching the billing period (e.g., monthly to annual) updates the price displays and savings message but does not trigger a live region announcement. Screen reader users are not notified that the prices they are viewing have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line N: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingAnnual); // or appropriate string inside setPeriod()

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing an FAQ item changes the visible content but does not announce the action to screen readers. Users may not realize the answer panel has opened or closed without a live region update.
issuesFound.fix:
Line N: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question)); // inside open()
Line N: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question)); // inside close()

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When form fields are marked invalid, the function updates aria-describedby to link to the error message but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1683: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` as the first line inside srUtils.markInvalid().

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, the trigger button's aria-expanded state is never updated to reflect the visibility of the associated panel. Assistive technologies cannot announce the expanded/collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2283: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside _openInstance(). Line 2297: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside _closeInstance().

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid reset
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing validation errors, the function removes the error description from aria-describedby but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. Screen readers may continue to announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`) after updating aria-describedby in srUtils.markValid().

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items expand and collapse using animateHeight and class toggles, but the trigger button does not have its aria-expanded attribute synchronized with the open/closed state. Screen readers cannot convey whether answer panels are visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3429: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside faqAccordion.open(). Line 3483: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` inside faqAccordion.close().

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product grid updates, the result count changes are not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed how many products match the selected filters.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3148: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.filterApplied ? STRINGS.filterApplied(s.activeCategory) : `Showing ${totalMatches} products`);`.

issuesFound.title: pricing billing toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) indicate active state via CSS classes only. They lack aria-pressed attributes, so screen readers cannot convey which billing period is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3758: Inside the forEach loop, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` after the toggleClass call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion non-button trigger missing Enter/Space activation
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ triggers may have role="button" assigned programmatically for non-button elements, but the keyboard handler only supports Arrow keys, Home, and End. It does not handle Enter or Space keys, preventing keyboard users from activating the accordion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3544: Add `case KEYS.ENTER:` and `case KEYS.SPACE:` before the default case, calling `toggle(item)` for the currently focused item.

issuesFound.title: pricing missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users switch between monthly and annual billing, the price changes are not announced to screen readers. Users are not informed that prices have updated or what the new amounts are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3738: After `_syncToggleState(period);`, add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button does not have an aria-controls attribute referencing the drawer element's ID. Screen readers cannot establish the programmatic relationship between the toggle button and the controlled navigation region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2242: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'nav-drawer'));` in mobileNav.init().

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ trigger buttons are not initialized with aria-expanded attributes matching their initial open/closed state. Screen readers cannot convey the default visibility of answer panels.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3582: Inside the forEach loop where initial states are applied, add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', shouldBeOpen ? 'true' : 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The navigation drawer opens and closes without announcing state changes to screen readers, despite having an announceToSR parameter. The parameter is unused for live region announcements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2165: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');` in open(). Line 2202: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` in close().

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus open and close without announcing their state changes to screen readers. Users navigating with keyboard or screen readers are not informed when submenus appear or disappear.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2287: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');` in _openInstance(). Line 2301: Add `liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');` in _closeInstance().

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items expand and collapse without announcing state changes to screen readers, despite STRINGS.faqOpened and STRINGS.faqClosed being defined in the configuration. The announceToSR parameter exists but is not used for live region updates.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3445: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : `${item.question} expanded`);` in open(). Line 3495: Add `if (announceToSR !== false) liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : `${item.question} collapsed`);` in close().

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open() and close() functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer and body classes but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1395: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1410: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance and _closeInstance functions in dropdownMenu manage the panel's hidden attribute and CSS classes but fail to toggle aria-expanded on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the dropdown state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1595: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1610: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not set aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item state and removes the hidden attribute from the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The close function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry that confuses screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3185: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input does not update aria-expanded dynamically
issuesFound.explanation: The siteSearch module's _open and _close functions update the internal state and visibility of the suggestion list but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the search input (_inputEl). Combobox inputs require this attribute to reflect the list state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2435: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 2445: setAttr(_inputEl, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons do not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function in the pricing module toggles the is-active class on period buttons ([data-period]) but does not update the aria-pressed attribute. These buttons act as a toggle group and must reflect their pressed state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2835: setAttr(btn, ARIA.PRESSED, String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator dynamic content not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _animate function in statCounterAnimator updates the textContent of the element with changing numbers but does not announce these changes to screen readers. Dynamic number updates should be contained within or announced to an aria-live region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3895: liveRegion.announce(`Stat updated to ${_format(cfg.target, cfg)}`);

issuesFound.title: planComparisonHighlighter visual change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: The highlightColumn function in planComparisonHighlighter toggles the is-featured class on table columns to indicate focus but does not announce this change. Screen reader users navigating the table will not know which column is highlighted.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2965: liveRegion.announce(`Column ${planId} highlighted`);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. This attribute is required to programmatically indicate the input's invalid state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1265: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not set aria-invalid to false
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function in srUtils removes the error description but does not reset aria-invalid to "false" or remove the attribute. The field may remain marked as invalid in the accessibility tree even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1280: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader visual hide does not set aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The hide and show functions in stickyHeader toggle the headerHidden class to visually hide the header but do not update aria-hidden. When visually hidden, the header should be removed from the accessibility tree to prevent confusion.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1865: _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true');
  Line 1875: _header.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _updateCountBadge function in productFilter updates the text content of the count badge but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users filtering products will not be informed of the new result count.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2585: liveRegion.announce(`${count} product${count !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);
