issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation fails, the inlineValidator marks fields as invalid by adding CSS classes and linking error messages via aria-describedby, but it never sets the aria-invalid attribute to true. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to identify invalid fields.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _renderResult, inside else block for invalid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` before or after the existing srUtils.markInvalid call.
  Line (in _renderResult, inside if block for valid): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` before or after the existing srUtils.markValid call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled via the open() and close() functions, which add and remove CSS classes to show or hide the panel. However, neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to reflect the open or closed state. Screen readers will not announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in mobileNav.open): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`
  Line (in mobileNav.close): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `removeClass(_drawer, CONFIG.navigation.openClass || CSS.open);`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the prices update visually with animation, but screen readers are not notified of the price change or the new billing period. Users may not realize the prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line (at end of setPeriod function): Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);` after the existing emit call.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels via _openInstance and _closeInstance, but these functions only manipulate the hidden attribute and CSS classes. They do not set aria-expanded on the trigger element to indicate the expanded state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _openInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line (in _closeInstance): Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `if (!inst.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When FAQ items are expanded or collapsed, the visual state changes and focus is managed, but there is no announcement to screen readers confirming the action (e.g., "Question expanded" or "Question collapsed"). This is especially important in multi-open mode where focus does not move.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in open function, before emit): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : \`\${item.question} expanded\`);`
  Line (in close function, before emit): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : \`\${item.question} collapsed\`);`

issuesFound.title: pricing billing period toggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle buttons (monthly/annual) visually indicate the active state via CSS classes, but the code does not update the aria-pressed attribute to communicate the pressed state to screen readers. This is required for toggle buttons that maintain a pressed state.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons visually indicate the active filter via CSS classes, but they lack the aria-pressed attribute. Since these buttons act as toggle buttons that remain pressed when selected, aria-pressed is required to convey the state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing aria-expanded initialization at page load
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation module initializes, it finds the trigger button and drawer elements but does not set the initial aria-expanded state to match the closed (default) state of the drawer. This leaves screen readers unaware of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in mobileNav.init, after _initialised = true;): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu missing aria-expanded initialization at page load
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a dropdown trigger and panel pair, the code ensures aria-haspopup and aria-controls are set, but it does not initialize aria-expanded to false for the closed state. This prevents screen readers from announcing the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in dropdownMenu.register, after setting aria-controls): Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the aria-describedby reference to error messages but does not reset aria-invalid to false or remove the attribute. This leaves the field marked as invalid for screen readers even after the error is corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line (at end of markValid function): Add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded on input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox that expands and collapses the suggestion listbox, but the aria-expanded attribute is never updated on the input element to reflect the open/closed state of the suggestions panel.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in _open function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of the function.
  Line (in _close function): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When the site search module initializes, it sets up the combobox pattern with aria-controls and aria-autocomplete, but it does not initialize aria-expanded to false for the closed state of the suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix: Line (in siteSearch.init, after suggestionListRenderer.mount): Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module declares a _trap variable for focus trapping but never initializes it with createFocusTrap. When the menu opens, focus is not trapped within the drawer, allowing users to tab out of the modal navigation and lose focus.
issuesFound.fix:
Line (in mobileNav.init, after _initialised = true): Add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
  Line (in mobileNav.open, after _lockScroll()): Add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus not returned to trigger on close
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, focus is not programmatically returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users are left with focus at the bottom of the DOM or in an unpredictable location.
issuesFound.fix: Line (at end of close function, after _unlockScroll()): Add `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open and mobileNav.close functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's open/closed state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2333: function open(announceToSR) { ... setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); ... }

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator not setting aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The inline validation controller visually marks fields as invalid using CSS classes and associates error messages via aria-describedby, but it never sets the aria-invalid attribute on the input fields. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to identify invalid fields programmatically.
issuesFound.fix: Line inlineValidator._renderResult (invalid branch): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` immediately after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldInvalid);`. Line inlineValidator._renderResult (valid branch): Add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` immediately after `addClass(field, CSS.fieldValid);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller opens and closes panels by manipulating CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but fails to set aria-expanded on the trigger button to indicate the expanded or collapsed state. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the dropdown state correctly.
issuesFound.fix: Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`. Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `if (!inst.open) inst.panel.setAttribute('hidden', '');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, the code sets CSS classes and emits events but fails to activate the focus trap that confines keyboard navigation within the drawer. Keyboard users can tab out of the drawer into the background content, which should be inaccessible while the drawer is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.open: After the line `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`, add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter missing live region announcement for results
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the visible product cards are updated via animation and the count badge is updated visually, but there is no announcement via the live region to inform screen reader users how many products match the current filter criteria or that the filter has been applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found matching your filters`);`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the open() function animates the panel and sets the open property, but only the close() function sets aria-expanded="false". The open() function never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen readers unaware that the content is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: pricing module missing live region announcement for price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled between monthly and annual, the price values update visually with animation, but the new prices are not announced to screen reader users via the live region. Users may not be aware that the content has changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line pricing.setPeriod: After the line `planDataParser.getAll().forEach(plan => _updatePlanCard(plan, period));`, add `liveRegion.announce(`Prices updated to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label} billing`);`.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-pressed for active state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons indicate the active category visually via CSS classes, but they do not use aria-pressed to communicate the pressed state to assistive technologies. Since these buttons act as toggle buttons (showing the active filter), aria-pressed is required to indicate which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter._syncTabAriaStates: Inside the forEach loop, after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`, add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox (role="combobox") with a suggestion listbox. The _open and _close functions manage the suggestion panel visibility but do not toggle aria-expanded on the input element to indicate whether the listbox is displayed. Screen readers cannot convey the expanded/collapsed state of the suggestions.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch._open: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the end of the function. Line siteSearch._close: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` at the end of the function.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is configured as a combobox with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but it lacks an initial aria-expanded="false" attribute to indicate that the listbox is initially collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line siteSearch.init: After the line `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`, add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing initial aria-expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is created or selected during initialization with the drawer in a closed state, but the code does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the trigger. Screen readers encounter the button without knowing it controls an expandable region.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: After the line `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);` and the null check, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion missing live region announcements for expand/collapse
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion expands and collapses content with animation, but does not announce the state change or the content of the newly revealed answer via the live region. Screen reader users may not be aware that the action succeeded or what content is now available.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu instance functions `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` manage the panel's `hidden` attribute but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button (`inst.trigger`). This breaks the disclosure pattern for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In `dropdownMenu._openInstance`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');`. In `dropdownMenu._closeInstance`, add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger

The `close` function correctly sets it to "false", creating an inconsistent state where screen readers only know when the item closes, not when it opens.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid fails to set aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markInvalid` utility function adds `aria-describedby` but does not set `aria-invalid="true"` on the invalid field. Form validation errors will not be properly announced as invalid by assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid fails to clear aria-invalid on fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `srUtils.markValid` utility function removes `aria-describedby` references but does not remove or reset the `aria-invalid` attribute. Fields that were previously invalid may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technology even after correction.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In `srUtils.markValid`, add `field.removeAttribute(ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing Enter/Space activation handler
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger's keydown handler (`_onTriggerKeydown`) only handles the Escape key. If the trigger is not a native `<button>` element, keyboard users cannot open or close the menu using Enter or Space keys.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In `mobileNav._onTriggerKeydown`, add a case for `KEYS.ENTER` and `KEYS.SPACE` that calls `toggle()`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap is declared but never initialized
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav` module declares a `_trap` variable and references it in `close()`, but never assigns `createFocusTrap(_drawer)` to it in `open()` or `init()`. Focus is not trapped within the mobile menu, allowing it to escape to the background content.
issuesFound.fix: Line N/A: In `mobileNav.open`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer); _trap.activate();`. In `mobileNav.close`, ensure `_trap.deactivate()` is called (already present but relies on initialization).

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed state

issuesFound.title: productFilter count update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product count badge text is updated, but the element (`_countBadge`) is not a live region and no announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the number of results has changed.

issuesFound.title: pricing savings message update not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close actions not announced via live region

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop click action not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Clicking the scroll-to-top button scrolls the page and moves focus, but does not announce the action. While focus movement helps, an explicit announcement confirms the action to screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-controls and initial aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger (`_trigger`) is not initialized with `aria-expanded="false"` or `aria-controls` pointing to the drawer (`_drawer`). The relationship between the button and the menu is not established on load.

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing Enter/Space activation
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tabs use `createRovingTabindex` for navigation but rely solely on click events for activation. If tabs are not native buttons, keyboard users cannot activate them with Enter or Space.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.processSvgIcons sets aria-hidden on all unnamed SVGs

issuesFound.fix: Line 720: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: HeroSection video toggle missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles the isPlaying state to play or pause the background video but uses only a dynamic aria-label. It lacks aria-pressed to indicate the current pressed/toggled state, which is the standard pattern for toggle buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 885: <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" aria-label={isPlaying ? 'Pause background video' : 'Play background video'} aria-pressed={isPlaying} onClick={handleTogglePlay} className="hero-section__video-toggle">

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle monthly button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly billing button is part of a toggle group that sets the billing period but lacks aria-pressed to indicate when it is the selected option. Screen reader users cannot perceive which billing period is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1010: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button toggles the isExpanded state for the mobile submenu but does not communicate this state via aria-expanded. Screen reader users cannot perceive whether the submenu is visible.

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
